== Total Death Count ==

The lead says the estimated death toll is 2,000 - 20,000 (dated Jan 10 and 13). However, numerous sources (from 4 days ago) says the casualty rate is "more than 500". Here are some examples:

https://www.telegraph.co.uk/world-news/2026/01/11/500-dead-bodies-iran-protests-crackdown/?msockid=03e855e8c168688311f0430ac0ab69a0

https://www.msn.com/en-us/news/world/more-than-500-killed-in-iran-unrest-rights-group-says/ar-AA1TZ7cj

https://www.yahoo.com/news/articles/more-500-people-killed-iran-210247354.html

Should this be substituted? Perhaps it should say the estimated death count ranges from 500 - 20,000? 

[[User:Wikieditor662|Wikieditor662]] ([[User talk:Wikieditor662|talk]]) 04:15, 16 January 2026 (UTC)

:All these sources cite an outdated figure from HRANA, rather than reporting it independently; we already have the updated figure from HRANA. [[User:9ninety|9ninety]] ([[User talk:9ninety|talk]]) 15:25, 20 January 2026 (UTC)
::Alright, thanks for letting me know. [[User:Wikieditor662|Wikieditor662]] ([[User talk:Wikieditor662|talk]]) 18:36, 20 January 2026 (UTC)
:I have credible sources which say over 90 million were killed. That's right, the cops killed everyone then themselves. All of Iran is dead.
:My credible sources are as credible as the propaganda."human rights" outlets funded by the US Government. F'ing hell guys, you can't ALL be this dim?
:Maybe add that the massacres stopped because Trump called Iran and told them to knock it off. He definitely seems to believe so, and he's the most credible source. Thank you for your attention to this matter! [[Special:Contributions/~2026-48769-7|~2026-48769-7]] ([[User talk:~2026-48769-7|talk]]) 02:24, 23 January 2026 (UTC)
::I don't think it's dimness, so much as inability or unwillingness to evaluate source reliability properly. [[User:Iskandar323|Iskandar323]] ([[User talk:Iskandar323|talk]]) 03:26, 23 January 2026 (UTC)
::disgusting comments like this really have no place here.
::I genuinely am disgusted by this behaviour [[User:Kane 1371|Kane 1371]] ([[User talk:Kane 1371|talk]]) 16:56, 3 February 2026 (UTC)
:::If you don't want people to express outrage at how transparent the propaganda nature of the content of this article is, then why don't you take the trouble to make it a little less transparent? [[Special:Contributions/~2026-13951-66|~2026-13951-66]] ([[User talk:~2026-13951-66|talk]]) 13:36, 4 March 2026 (UTC)
::~30,000 sounds like a great deal of political bias to justify the deployment of an illegal war. [[Special:Contributions/~2026-14095-33|~2026-14095-33]] ([[User talk:~2026-14095-33|talk]]) 00:10, 5 March 2026 (UTC)

== Where did the 30k estimate come from? ==

The highest number thrown around is 12k, which has been largely reported only by iran international and CBC. This page loses any credibility reporting imaginary numbers [[Special:Contributions/~2026-36210-8|~2026-36210-8]] ([[User talk:~2026-36210-8|talk]]) 12:09, 17 January 2026 (UTC)

:Certain "alleged" propagandists have flooded all Wikipedia pages concerning the riots and are now just magically inventing casualty numbers out of thin air. It went from 70 to 200 to 2000 to 12000 to 20000 and now 30000 killed in only a few days. That is more than double the amount of civilians killed in 4 years of the Russo-Ukrainian war. [[User:SwedishDutch|SwedishDutch]] ([[User talk:SwedishDutch|talk]]) 17:58, 17 January 2026 (UTC)
::Don't be absurd - more than that number of civilians died in the two months of the [[Siege of Mariupol]] alone. [[User:Fig wright|Fig]] ([[User talk:Fig wright|talk]]) 18:38, 17 January 2026 (UTC)
:::Over two months of a siege, the UN was able to confirm the deaths of only above 1,000 civilians killed in the Siege of Mariupol, although conceding it was ''probably'' "thousands higher". Of course the Ukrainians and their allies, who have every intention of portraying the Russian forces as merely a barbaric horde, claim an unsubstantiated civilian death toll in the tens of thousands, and of course claim that it was all the Russians' doing, despite widespread civilian support for the Russian invasion in the occupied parts of Ukraine (soon to be recognized as Russian oblasts in a peace treaty), verified by the numbers who fled ''east'', and the fact that Mariupol, even Kherson, had a substantial "fifth column" that provided material support to the Russian invaders. 
:::Nonetheless, that is an actual war zone, and what does it say about the Russians that in two months of siege warfare, they killed fewer civilians than the Iranians allegedly killed in a matter of days? Are wikipedia editors ''that'' credulous? This is a bad case of one-sided skepticism where some suspension of judgement might be more helpful toward fulfilling wikipedia's mission of providing accurate information to the public, not to mention toward defusing Jingoistic public discourse in the English speaking world, premised on the reports of US-backed NGOs that are politically aligned with the State Department's long-standing project of overthrowing the government of Iran, which is driving us toward a potentially nuclear confrontation. I would hope the threat of a nuclear holocaust might override some of the patriotic ethos here. [[User:Situwannabe|Situwannabe]] ([[User talk:Situwannabe|talk]]) 17:27, 17 February 2026 (UTC)
:A much better source is needed than CBN or Iran International for these upper estimates. The page should be looking to figures provided in the own voice of serious, mainstream news sources. One GREL RSP at a minimum. [[User:Iskandar323|Iskandar323]] ([[User talk:Iskandar323|talk]]) 19:31, 17 January 2026 (UTC)
::This page is propaganda in real time, saying “alleged” does not give you an excuse to throw any number you want. It took over a year for people to reach such numbers in Gaza but for Iran media outlets are so easily throwing around exponentially increasing numbers. What are the mechanism to report this page to Wikipedia because at this point this point this page is a sorry excuse of an article. [[Special:Contributions/~2026-36210-8|~2026-36210-8]] ([[User talk:~2026-36210-8|talk]]) 19:39, 17 January 2026 (UTC)
:::"It took Gaza a year, but Iran supposedly did it in a week! It's unrealistic" Sorry, but we're not playing "Genocide Olympics" here, we're trying to collectivize information for the internet, and information from Iran more or less confirms that this was a massacre on an unimaginable scale. 
:::Realistic? Obviously not, but what is? Would the Holocaust be realistic to someone in 1910? Would the Gaza genocide be realistic to someone in 2020? By denying objective evidence that has already been provided, you are going down the steps of genocide denial (although in this case it's Politicide, not genocide) that being "Denial". 
:::That said, your objectivity in this matter is rather suspect. You seem more angry about the fact that a political massacre DARED to seemingly outpace Gaza's genocide, than you are concerned with the fact that the Iranian government seemingly just did the largest political massacre in recent history.  [[User:Reaperz 2020|Reaperz 2020]] ([[User talk:Reaperz 2020|talk]]) 20:43, 22 January 2026 (UTC)
::::Lol it's confirmed by propaganda. You're a true genius buddy. [[Special:Contributions/~2026-48769-7|~2026-48769-7]] ([[User talk:~2026-48769-7|talk]]) 02:25, 23 January 2026 (UTC)
:CBS and Iran International are cited throughout these articles as [[WP:RS]], but as CBN seems to be contentious, I'm removing it and the tag, and restoring the article.--[[User:Ronnnaldo7|Ronnnaldo7]] ([[User talk:Ronnnaldo7|talk]]) 20:36, 17 January 2026 (UTC)
::{{re|Ronnnaldo7}} Sorry, no the [[Pat Robertson]]-founded [[Christian Broadcasting Network]] is certainly not RS especially for a contentious topic like this. This has been made clear in more than enough discussions at [[WP:RSN]]: [[Wikipedia:Reliable sources/Noticeboard/Archive 471#Christian Broadcasting Network]], [[Wikipedia:Reliable sources/Noticeboard/Archive 343#CBN - Christian Broadcasting Network]]. Moreover these figures are not from the CBN, it is citing unnamed reports as a source. The entire CBN article is entirely not acceptable for the [[Wikipedia:CT/IRP]] contentious topic space (or generally for that matter).
::I have as such undone your edits at the main protest article as well. You should find better sources for the 30,000 figure (supposedly from 'some' reports that the CBN cites), otherwise this isn't going in. [[User:Gotitbro|Gotitbro]] ([[User talk:Gotitbro|talk]]) 20:47, 17 January 2026 (UTC)
:::Right, which is why I removed the CBN content from this article. Thank you for pointing me to the CBN RS discussion; that's what I was looking for and was struggling to find as it looks to be in the archives. [[User:Ronnnaldo7|Ronnnaldo7]] ([[User talk:Ronnnaldo7|talk]]) 20:52, 17 January 2026 (UTC)
::::While CBS is globally considered a credible source even though their figure is based on a single anonymous contact, I will question Iran International report as their sources are a clear stretch and an attempt to get breaking news out there, based on their articles their sources include someone close to the Supreme National Security Council, two sources in the presidential office, and several sources from within the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps. Given these numbers contradict the official statements from the same source, I request that the article avoids using Iran International unless they start providing more credible sources for their informations, as currently it appears as a blatant attempt to fabricate breaking news. [[Special:Contributions/~2026-36210-8|~2026-36210-8]] ([[User talk:~2026-36210-8|talk]]) 21:40, 17 January 2026 (UTC)
:::::We have an ongoing discussion for Iran Intl. at [[Wikipedia:Reliable sources/Noticeboard#Is Iran International a reliable source?]]. While I wouldn't be too sure of its reporting under [[Bari Weiss]], we list [[WP:CBS]] as RS. Though I agree that anon sources are something we shouldn't be displaying so prominently or perhaps at all.
:::::But until we have a firmer grip on the numbers from much more stable academic sources and the like, this is what we have for now. [[User:Gotitbro|Gotitbro]] ([[User talk:Gotitbro|talk]]) 03:52, 18 January 2026 (UTC)
::HRANA appears to have a very good reputation by all regards, and is well referenced by RSP, so it's unclear why any of these rough guestimates in the '000s (and attributed to activists) are being privileged over the one human rights group with a seemingly rigorous and exacting approach. [[User:Iskandar323|Iskandar323]] ([[User talk:Iskandar323|talk]]) 04:14, 18 January 2026 (UTC)
:::There is also the issue of randomly giving preference/listing first higher deaths tolls against enwiki guidleines for ranges or precedent. I started a discussion about that here: [[Talk:2025–2026 Iranian protests#Casualties]]. [[User:Gotitbro|Gotitbro]] ([[User talk:Gotitbro|talk]]) 05:20, 18 January 2026 (UTC)
:::HRANA/HRAI get every single cent they get from the National Endowment for Democracy, i.e. the United States government. A very good reputation indeed.
:::Anyway, the human rights agency I just founded in my head says 200 million dead. Please adjust the article accordingly. Trump will confirm, surely. [[Special:Contributions/~2026-48769-7|~2026-48769-7]] ([[User talk:~2026-48769-7|talk]]) 02:29, 23 January 2026 (UTC)
::::HRANA actually has a methodology, which immediately makes it more useful than Iran International or any of the other rumour-mongering sources. [[User:Iskandar323|Iskandar323]] ([[User talk:Iskandar323|talk]]) 03:31, 23 January 2026 (UTC)
:::I have answered you in Iran intl RS discussion.
:::However, here we go, according to HARANA themselves (currently at well above 12000) they are sure the death tolls are above what their final numbers land on as they cannot get every single name.
:::Their work is trying to get every single name possible, not a precise estimates of all dead as that simply is not possible when dealing with a regime that has a history of digging mass graves both in Iran and Syria. == Death toll estimates table ==
<!-- START PIN -->{{Pin message|14:56, 2 February 2029 (UTC)}}<!-- [[User:DoNotArchiveUntil]] 14:56, 10 March 2026 (UTC) -->{{User:ClueBot III/DoNotArchiveUntil|1773154577}}<!-- END PIN -->
The table on this talk page section is to help make it easier for editing debates on the death toll to converge on the preferred sources and estimates of the numbers of protesters and security forces killed in the protests. Without a table, it gets very confusing when editors introduce citation loops of A cited B who cited C who cited D and then attribute the estimate to A. Feel free to update this table independently of discussion within this section. [[User:Boud|Boud]] ([[User talk:Boud|talk]]) 22:42, 23 January 2026 (UTC)

Feel free to replace updates by a given source (e.g. HRANA) by the same source's more recent estimate. [[User:Boud|Boud]] ([[User talk:Boud|talk]]) 22:42, 23 January 2026 (UTC)  (Or do other updates or add new sources.) [[User:Boud|Boud]] ([[User talk:Boud|talk]]) 19:27, 24 January 2026 (UTC)

===Discussion===
The table aims to include estimates that have some chance of being in editing debates, including those that are rather weak, fuzzy sources, for the purpose of this (or related) talk pages. [[User:Boud|Boud]] ([[User talk:Boud|talk]]) 22:42, 23 January 2026 (UTC)

== '50,000' - 80,000+ killed? ==

Given the latest (1.25.26), from Iran Intl. That over 36,500 died in just the 48 hours Jan 8-9, it might be highly believable that the total deaths, during the entire protesting period is 50,000.
These are sources that claim the 50k toll. 

This is copied from the blogger in [https://justsayingitoutloloud.blogspot.com/2025/09/the-genocide-lie.html the genocide lie] 

* Other Voices: Please do not look away from what is happening in Iran.
15 Jan 2026 — ... "Iran face violence, writes this member of the Iranian community in ... Some say the death toll is much higher, maybe even 50,000 people."
[https://www.nsnews.com/opinion/other-voices-do-not-look-away-from-what-is-happening-in-iran-11746524]

* 'Iran must be rebuilt on the foundation of dignity, coexistence, and truth.'
Vahid Beheshti. The Jerusalem Post. January 23, 2026.
"Unconfirmed sources inside Iran speak of up to 50,000 killed across more than 180 cities."
[https://www.jpost.com/opinion/article-884297]

* 'Iran's exiled prince accuses West of 'throwing protesters under bus' in scathing rant.'
GBN News. January 25, 2026. — "Mr Pahlavi said the reports he is receiving total the number killed to be in excess of 50,000."
[https://www.gbnews.com/news/world/iran-exiled-prince-west-protesters][[User:Willysbillys|Willysbillys]] ([[User talk:Willysbillys|talk]]) 04:25, 26 January 2026 (UTC)

:The only [[WP:RS]] here is the Jerusalem Post. The 50k figure seems to be coming from Pahlavi, see this interview in the Sunday Times [https://www.thetimes.com/world/middle-east/article/reza-pahlavi-iran-crown-prince-interview-r9dbng5qb] -- this would need to be corroborated by independent RS doing their own reporting to warrant inclusion. [[User:DiodotusNicator|DiodotusNicator]] ([[User talk:DiodotusNicator|talk]]) 05:10, 26 January 2026 (UTC)
::I appreciate your comment. This [https://www.nsnews.com/opinion/other-voices-do-not-look-away-from-what-is-happening-in-iran-11746524] and the JPost [https://www.jpost.com/opinion/article-884297] pieces were published before Pahlavi's quote cited in ''Times'' 1.25.26. Nor do they mention Pahlavi. (Author, activist Vahid Beheshti, per ''JPost'' is "a political activist and founder of Iran Front for the Revival of Law and National Sovereignty." So I went to [https://www.iranfront.org/boardmembers Iran Front, Board members], Pahlavi isn't there). <!-- Template:Unsigned --><small class="autosigned">— Preceding [[Wikipedia:Signatures|unsigned]] comment added by [[User:Willysbillys|Willysbillys]] ([[User talk:Willysbillys#top|talk]] • [[Special:Contributions/Willysbillys|contribs]]) 06:03, 26 January 2026 (UTC)</small>
:::The [http://archive.today/2026.01.29-194935/https://www.jpost.com/opinion/article-884297 JPost] article not only uses weak wording {{tq|Unconfirmed sources inside Iran speak of up to 50,000 killed}} ("up to" is consistent with any value such as 5 or 50 or 500 or 5000 or 50,000), and in the immediately following sentence cites a Wikipedia-deprecated source [[WP:DAILYMAIL]]. So overall it's a weak source, at least for the death count in this particular event. [[User:Boud|Boud]] ([[User talk:Boud|talk]]) 20:00, 29 January 2026 (UTC)
Journalist[https://www.huffpost.com/author/shirin-sadeghi] Shirin Sadeghi, already on 1.22.26 posted[https://archive.is/6V0gp]:
“This Dr. conservatively estimates 60,000 killed, 360,000 injured and at least 1 million directly affected.”
And:
"Turlockers continue to show support for Iranian protesters." Turlock Journal. 1.28.26 [https://www.turlockjournal.com/news/local/turlockers-continue-to-show-support-for-iranian-protesters/]
''"it’s over 60,000 that have been killed,” said Turlock’s...''
I guess we won't know the exact numbers under current dictatorship?[[User:Willysbillys|Willysbillys]] ([[User talk:Willysbillys|talk]]) 09:56, 26 January 2026 (UTC)
:The strongest source there that I see is ''The Sunday Times''<ref name="SunTimes_throwing_under_a_bus">{{cite Q|Q137901091|url-access=subscription}}</ref> which I've added to the talk page table at [[Talk:2026 Iran massacres#Death toll estimates table]], and I've added it to the ''body'' of this article ''with attribution''. Given Pahlavi's prominent role as a major participant, I haven't added it to the lead or infobox. I recommend ''seeking [[WP:CONSENSUS]]'' here on the talk page before adding it to the lead or infobox.{{pb}}The 60k estimate ''appears'' to be attributed to Amir Parasta, who was earlier seen as reliable by ''Time''. However, we would need a RS for quoting Parasta's update. I haven't checked all the URLs above, so I might have missed a good one. [[User:Boud|Boud]] ([[User talk:Boud|talk]]) 17:05, 26 January 2026 (UTC)
: Many believe the death toll will soon exceed 50,000. For now, we should rely on the latest figures from reliable sources such as Iran International and Time. Including outdated figures is not suitable for encyclopedia. <small style="border:1px solid #444;padding:1px;background:#183693">  [[User:Arash.pt|<strong style="color:#FFF">ARASH PT</strong>]] [[User_talk:Arash.pt|<span style="color:white;"> talk </span>]] </small> 14:41, 28 January 2026 (UTC)

::[[Darya Safai]], from her Sunday 1.25.26 speech  [https://www.facebook.com/SafaiDarya/videos/my-speech-on-sunday-was-in-english-because-through-our-broken-hearts-we-were-spe/2168984433507362/] "In just three days, we lost 60,000 beautiful young lives." <!-- Template:Unsigned --><small class="autosigned">— Preceding [[Wikipedia:Signatures|unsigned]] comment added by [[User:Willysbillys|Willysbillys]] ([[User talk:Willysbillys#top|talk]] • [[Special:Contributions/Willysbillys|contribs]]) 22:27, 29 January 2026 (UTC)</small>
:::A personal opinion on a generally unreliable source [[WP:RSPFACEBOOK]], with no hint (per the quote immediately above, here on this talk page) regarding ''where'' the information actually comes from is only usable in a ''Reactions'' section or article. [[Darya Safai]] is a notable person, but as far as I know, we're probably not taking any sources seriously that just state a number without saying ''anything'' about the method of collecting the data and the source of the data. [[User:Boud|Boud]] ([[User talk:Boud|talk]]) 12:27, 30 January 2026 (UTC) ''(minor fix [[User:Boud|Boud]] ([[User talk:Boud|talk]]) 12:30, 30 January 2026 (UTC))''
It is the reason I didn't edit the page, for the need to discuss these first.

'''Origin and/or those who claim death toll: 80,000 or more'''

* [[UN]] Special Rapporteur on the situation of human rights in the Islamic Republic of Iran, [[Mai Sato]] receives reports of (up to) 80,000 deaths. (Published on 1.20.26).
ONU recebe relatos de 80 mil mortes no Irã - Revista Oeste. UN receives reports of 80,000 deaths in Iran - Oeste Magazine. Jan 22, 2026 [https://revistaoeste.com/mundo/onu-recebe-relatos-de-80-mil-mortes-no-ira/]
abcnews_au: 📍 Is Iran's massacre of civilians crimes against humanity? 
📸 UN Special Rapporteur on the situation of human rights in the Islamic Republic of Iran, Mai Sato, gave an exclusive sit-down interview with ABC News reporter Nassim Khadem.
🕒 Published 5:30pm AEST, January 20, 2026
[...]
"I would put the minimum estimates to be 5,000 plus. That's sort of the more conservative organisations reporting that figure. But also, last day or so, Iranian authorities have actually said it will be around 5,000 deaths. 
So I think that is the minimum. And then I've also received reports up to 20,000. And as you say, they have been largely through courageous doctors with access to Starlink being able to provide information of people who they've received in hospital but not every region in the hospital, not every doctor has the courage or the capacity to be able to provide that information. 
So if what we are receiving it estimates at 60,000 or 80,000 I think the actual number once we have more information, will be significantly higher."
[https://www.instagram.com/reel/DTuWFi3kWoE/]
UN receives reports of 80,000 deaths in Iran - Oeste Magazine.
Jan 22, 2026 
[https://revistaoeste.com/mundo/onu-recebe-relatos-de-80-mil-mortes-no-ira/]


* The claim on [[Mohsen Hashemi]] 1.23.26:
"According to received information,
during a private meeting of several so-called reformist figures, Mohsen Hashemi stated that based on estimates by military institutions (IRGC), the number of people killed in the recent crackdown has exceeded 80,000."
[https://x.com/__Injaneb96/status/2014484450315293173 Jan 23, 2026]
[https://x.com/Rppress0/status/2014915823543189835 Jan 24, 2026]
Protests in Iran, day 27: Khamenei cancelled Friday prayers. Fearing to meet his 72 virgins.
By: Arnt Jensvoll. 24. January 2026.
...Sana Ebrahimi: “The most recent report I’ve received, from someone with contact with sources within the government, suggests that the regime’s own estimate of the number of people killed exceeds 80,000.– In a meeting with Mohsen Hashemi, he reportedly stated that the total number of people killed, based on military estimates, is over 80,000.
[https://www.document.news/news/2026/01/protests-in-iran-day-27-khamenei-cancelled-friday-prayers-fearing-to-meet-his-72-virgins]



* LBC cites 45k-80k (1.25.26)
Iran execute protesters in hospital beds, with fears the death toll now surpassing 30,000 in ongoing crackdown.
Natasha Devon. 25 Jan 2026.

"The regime provides no information on whether they are detained or have been killed. Based on the available evidence, a more realistic estimate is at least 45,000 people death -some people estimate it to 80,000."
[https://www.lbc.co.uk/article/iran-execute-prisoners-hospital-tehrans-crackdown-5HjdRHb_2/]



* LBC interviews 
''ISTO'' organization. (1.26.26):
''Iran’s January 2026 Massacre EXPOSED | 45,000–80,000 Dead in 48 Hours | LBC Interview. Paul Smith - Options Trader 🍒 and Iran Sanctions & Trade Observatory (ISTO)''.
Jan 26, 2026.[https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=XlStk8sEOCc]. 


* Then (1.30.26) by the IRANO org. Irano.info
[https://www.instagram.com/reel/DUIKYmUDegC/]
[[User:Willysbillys|Willysbillys]] ([[User talk:Willysbillys|talk]]) 01:45, 3 February 2026 (UTC)

:: I don't see in this list any new sources of rumours or new claims of a death toll of 80k.{{pb}}''[[Revista Oeste]]'' A statement to ABC News, apparently translated to Portuguese and then back to English as {{tq|If what we're getting is estimates of 60,000 or 80,000, ...}} is ''not'' a statement of an actual estimate (it sounds like a response to a suggestion/question by the interviewer; in any case, it's not a claim). [[WP:RSPINSTAGRAM]] is not a source. [12] and [14] are two copies of a single URL.{{pb}}Twitter is not a source, and we already have the Sana Ebrahimi/[[Mohsen Hashemi Rafsanjani]] claim at [[Talk:2026 Iran massacres#Table]], clearly labelled as a sourceless [[WP:RSTWITTER]] rumour only, so I see nothing new there.{{pb}}The words {{tq|some people estimate it to 80,000}} on LBC are just evidence that the rumour exists, not an actual claim. [[WP:RSPYOUTUBE]] and [[WP:RSPINSTAGRAM]] are not sources. [[User:Boud|Boud]] ([[User talk:Boud|talk]]) 21:45, 4 February 2026 (UTC)

{{reflist-talk}}

== Why isn't this categorized as a crime of Extermination? ==
https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Extermination_(crime) [[Special:Contributions/~2026-38675-7|~2026-38675-7]] ([[User talk:~2026-38675-7|talk]]) 12:10, 30 January 2026 (UTC)
:Because nobody has done the work for Wikipedia of providing a [[WP:RS|reliable source]] for that description. Wikipedia is edited by volunteers.{{pb}}If you can give us a [[WP:RS]] that describes the massacres as likely qualifying as an [[extermination (crime)|extermination]], preferably a human rights organisation or a legal expert or an organisation recognised as having legal expertise in [[international criminal law]], then that could be added to the [[2026 Iran massacres#Types of atrocities]] section. So far we have two sources in that section for likely [[crimes against humanity]]. Extermination is a crime against humanity under [[:s:Rome Statute of the International Criminal Court#Article 7: Crimes against humanity|Article 7.1(b) of the Rome Statute]].{{pb}}Any sources? [[User:Boud|Boud]] ([[User talk:Boud|talk]]) 12:44, 30 January 2026 (UTC)
::I think the reality is it's such a rarely cited crime against humanity that most media/orgs forget it exists, most comparative crimes are either hyperbolized as genocide or downplayed as a massacre. [[Special:Contributions/~2026-38675-7|~2026-38675-7]] ([[User talk:~2026-38675-7|talk]]) 17:32, 30 January 2026 (UTC)
:::+1 for crime against humanity with {{diff|2026 Iran massacres|1335896761|1335824877|the Iranian intellectuals' statement}}. You're welcome to try to convince lawyers or human rights orgs to use the term ''extermination'' specifically. [[User:Boud|Boud]] ([[User talk:Boud|talk]]) 21:04, 31 January 2026 (UTC)
::I think when Payam Akhavan says this is a crime on the level of Holocaust and calls it extermination and going as far as implying the only reason he is not calling it genocide is the current definition, it is very telling.
::https://m.youtube.com/watch?v=LMFrrP0ilQw&t=1715s&pp=2AGzDZACAQ%3D%3D [[User:Kane 1371|Kane 1371]] ([[User talk:Kane 1371|talk]]) 18:20, 3 February 2026 (UTC)

== Where is Iran Intl's Truth Registration Map? ==

Iran International [https://archive.today/2026.02.03-112524/https://www.iranintl.com/en/202602021839 said] on 3 Feb {{tq|So far, the verified details of 1,141 individuals have been published on the "Truth Registration Map" on Iran International's website, and this process continues daily}} but did not actually give a URL, despite the statement actually being on Iran International's website ... My guess is the only reason for not linking was that the "Truth Registration Map" is only in Persian (or maybe the same text gets posted on social media?). In any case, that would be highly relevant as an external link here. What's the URL for the "Truth Registration Map"? 

It would also be useful if someone can give us the URL for the government's official list of victims.

Both of these should be uncontroversial as [[WP:EXTERNALLINKS]].

So far we only have https://iranvictims.com in ''External links'' for websites that have published their [[casualty recording|detailed lists]]. [[User:Boud|Boud]] ([[User talk:Boud|talk]]) 11:59, 3 February 2026 (UTC)

:here it is [https://javidnaman.iranintl.com/] [[User:Kane 1371|Kane 1371]] ([[User talk:Kane 1371|talk]]) 17:41, 3 February 2026 (UTC)
::Excellent. Added. If someone from ''Iran International'' is reading this (or if someone wants to contact ''Iran Intl'', feel free to), then I would strongly recommend adding a [[comma-separated values|CSV]] downloadable file like https://iranvictims.com/victims.csv to the javidnaman.iranintl.com website. That would make it easier for people to compare the different lists. [[User:Boud|Boud]] ([[User talk:Boud|talk]]) 18:12, 3 February 2026 (UTC)

== Iranian govt is an unreliable source for the death count ==

{{ping|Ardehali}} In {{diff|2026 Iran massacres|prev|1336298710|this edit at 00:44, 3 February 2026}} and {{diff|2026 Iran massacres|prev|1336298710|this edit at 03:51, 4 February 2026}} you introduced the count of 3,117 deaths by the Iranian government. However, the counts by the Iranian government [[2026 Iran massacres#Iranian government|are disinformation]]; in other words, the Iranian government is not a [[WP:RS]] for counts of deaths in these protests. Please read [[2026 Iran massacres#Iranian government|the relevant section in the body of the article]]. This is a [[WP:CTOP]]. Please do not restore the Iranian government [[disinformation]] on the death count to the infobox unless you first obtain consensus among editors here on the talk page. [[User:Boud|Boud]] ([[User talk:Boud|talk]]) 22:04, 4 February 2026 (UTC) ''(clarify [[User:Boud|Boud]] ([[User talk:Boud|talk]]) 22:29, 4 February 2026 (UTC))''

:Why is the Iranian government unreliable, but "human rights organizations" sponsored by governments which are openly hostile to the Iranian government considered reliable? HRANA for example is approvingly cited as a "reliable source", yet, they are on the payroll of the US federal government via the "National Endowment for Democracy", and based in Fairfax, Virginia, mere miles away from Foggy Bottom. [[User:Situwannabe|Situwannabe]] ([[User talk:Situwannabe|talk]]) 17:06, 17 February 2026 (UTC)
::{{tq|Why is the Iranian government unreliable}}
::I am fascinated by the amount of [[WP:SEALION]]ing around this question. Do you think Soviet state media would have been a reliable source on the [[Katyn massacre]]? [[User:DiodotusNicator|DiodotusNicator]] ([[User talk:DiodotusNicator|talk]]) 11:40, 18 February 2026 (UTC)
:The Iranian government shouldn't be cited directly, but when reliable secondary sources cite the Iranian government, then yes, wikipedia should report on that. Sources that report on the Iranian government death toll include: [https://www.theguardian.com/world/2026/feb/01/calls-grow-in-iran-for-independent-inquiry-into-protest-death-toll The Guardian], [https://time.com/7357635/more-than-30000-killed-in-iran-say-senior-officials/ Time], [https://www.pbs.org/newshour/world/more-than-7000-dead-in-irans-crackdown-on-protests-activists-say PBS], [https://www.washingtonpost.com/world/2026/02/03/iran-protests-deaths-crackdown/ Washington Post], [https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2026/2/5/questions-after-irans-government-releases-victim-list-in-protest-killings Al-Jazeera], [https://www.ctvnews.ca/world/article/irans-top-diplomat-issues-most-direct-threat-yet-to-us-as-crackdown-over-protests-squeezes-nation/ CTV News] etc.'''[[User talk:Vice regent|VR]]''' <sub>(Please [[Template:Ping|ping]] on reply)</sub> 11:23, 28 February 2026 (UTC)

== Image for casualties section ==

{{ping|Gotitbro}} In {{diff|2026 Iran massacres|prev|1336955023|this edit}}, you removed the link to [[:File:Mamlekate Telegram 2026-01-26 98556 1.jpg]], with the edit summary {{tq|image of unknown provenance, Telegram is of course not RS}}. Please see [[MOS:PHOTOS]]. The primary need is that {{tq|images must be significant and relevant in the topic's context, not primarily decorative. Each image in an article should have a clear and unique illustrative purpose and serve as an important illustrative aid to understanding the subject.}} We don't use images as ''sources'' for [[WP:VERIFIABILITY]]; we use them to ''illustrate'' an article. The fact that the network of activists who posted the image protect their anonymity is not a problem, unless there's some doubt about whether the image is faked or [[AI slop]], but my impression is that AI slop typically gets refused by Commons.

To put things another way: we don't want [[WP:SPS]] as sources, but many photos on Commons that get used on Wikipedia ''are'' self-published by non-Wikipedia-notable people.

{{s|It would be good if we had more info about the image, but to me it seems reasonable to use it: it is a good illustration of the casualties (on a tiny scale).}}

I propose that anyone interested add arguments here for or against using the image in this article, after browsing through [[MOS:PHOTOS]]. [[User:Boud|Boud]] ([[User talk:Boud|talk]]) 23:31, 6 February 2026 (UTC)

:Sorry, I was confused between image ID numbers. For [[:File:Mamlekate Telegram 2026-01-26 98556 1.jpg]], I tend to think that this image is usable, with a caption such as ''Woman killed by live fire, [[Chalus, Iran|Chalus]], 7 January 2026.'' [[User:Boud|Boud]] ([[User talk:Boud|talk]]) 00:30, 7 February 2026 (UTC)
::Do we have a reliable source on the "live fire"? Perhaps if a similar image appeared in a RS, we could then make the connection.'''[[User talk:Vice regent|VR]]''' <sub>(Please [[Template:Ping|ping]] on reply)</sub> 11:28, 28 February 2026 (UTC)
:This is a [[Wikipedia:Contentious topics/Iranian politics]] article, there is absolutely no way that an image attributed to a no name Telegram channel is going in. Annonymity is not a problem as long as the provenance/source is an RS or the publisher has uploaded it themselves; not random Telegram channels. This would be the first article at enwiki where images sourced to unreliable Telegram channels are being used.
:As for Commons, at the main article itself we likely have an AI-altered image inserted and dishonestly uploaded (certainly not owned by the uploader) to Commons, [[:File:Iran protests 8 January 2026.jpg]]. So an upload to Commons is simply not rationale enough for inclusion, including for reasons of COPYVIO and others above. Whatever license the channel maybe publishing it under, it neither owns them nor do we have any idea whether any of it is veritable. There are more than enough images of the protests at Commons, we should not be using unreliable gory images here in lieu of them. [[User:Gotitbro|Gotitbro]] ([[User talk:Gotitbro|talk]]) 04:30, 7 February 2026 (UTC)
::For the record, {{diff|2026 Iran massacres|prev|1337046071|you've also removed}} the link to [[:File:Mamlekate Telegram 2026-01-27 98558 1.jpg]].{{pb}}Copyright questions of images should be sorted out at Commons. The uploader is a well-known [[w:fa:ویژه:اختیارات_کاربر/Darafsh|fa.Wikipedia admin]], {{u|Darafsh}}, who is not an RS for textual type information, but as above, RSes are not required for [[MOS:PHOTOS|images]]. [[Flickr]] is not a reliable source either, but many images from Flickr are accepted on Commons, and [[:File:Chapel for the sake of Sacred great martyr Ekaterina.jpg|used on the Wikipedias]]. I do see a deletion proposal at [[:Commons:Commons:Deletion requests/File:Iran protests 8 January 2026.jpg]], so I propose any suggestions of copyright violations or AI manipulation be sorted out there.{{pb}}Regarding {{tq|gory images}}, please see [[WP:NOTCENSORED]]. [[User:Boud|Boud]] ([[User talk:Boud|talk]]) 10:54, 7 February 2026 (UTC)
:::NOTCENSORED yes, but to be wary of contentious images with regards to their veracity also yes. Flickr is a great example here, as in no case would we be doling out random images from Commons/Flickr on enwiki articles without regards to the creator. If they are anon or unattributable, highly unlikely they would be inserted.
:::Veritable images of the massacre(s) exist from non-free sources (AP etc.), and if they are seen to be essential a fair use case can be easily made. We needn't rely on random Telegram channels and the like. [[User:Gotitbro|Gotitbro]] ([[User talk:Gotitbro|talk]]) 14:55, 10 February 2026 (UTC)
